summary: This cluster investigates the population dynamics and life history ecology of the red alga *Chondrus crispus* (Gigartinales, Rhodophyta), specifically focusing on the spatial and temporal distribution of its gametophytic and tetrasporophytic life history phases. Research conducted in Nova Scotia over a thirty-year period (1993-2011) analyzed over 2,800 thalli, using the presence of kappa- and lambda-carrageenan to distinguish between the two phases. A consistent pattern of gametophyte dominance (a ratio of 3:1 or greater) was documented, with significant fluctuations at specific sites linked to recovery from disturbance and changes in substrate composition. The findings indicate a stable natural range for gametophyte ratios in the region, where lower ratios signify post-disturbance recovery and highlight the role of carpospores in regeneration.
entity_sample: lower part of the C. crispus zone
